was really just a bandwidth constraint.

We have a data scientist like specifically looking at the sources that each prompt is citing and mentioning and we're surfacing those within the check that app as well so that you'll be able to see like these are the kinds of articles that AI is referencing so that we can replicate that and actually build a little bit more of like a moat around the organic search by answering the questions in formats and like with content that we know AI is searching for when people are asking those evaluation questions.

Yeah.
And I think the story there really is that you don't need to.
I mean, you're getting there without it, but if you get the stuff to do it, there's, you know, room there to grow.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Okay.
Okay.

So basically, just to sum up, kind of, like, what we learned here is that, you know, as these clusters mature, we start to see a leveling off of, you know, how that growth is happening, you know, whereas we're seeing with, like, you know, especially some of the newer clusters, that's kind of still kind of, you know, compounding there, while franchise taxes kind of leveled off at about a 5% growth rate.
And that just basically means that different clusters just need different treatment, right?
It's the difference between creating new content and maintaining the content and the traffic that you already have.
And then with AI visibility, that's a defensible moat.
Like we can say that it's, hey, we don't have to play catch up.
We're out in front kind of leading the way a little bit where we can.
And so we need to continue doing what we're doing and continue to build on that.
And then the ROI of refreshes really have kind of exceeded expectations.
So, you know, as we're scaling just those 100, I think it was 110 refreshes that the team did, like we're able to kind of keep us scaling at a pretty good pace.
So, you know, that first content we helped with and even the content you created, that still performs because we've maintained kind of those individual articles.
So basically, you know, just traffic problem has been solved essentially, right?
So we're kind of saying now for phase two, like we focus, we shift from that from coverage to where we can pull in leads.
Yep.
Okay.
Um, so one of the things that we learned in the process of.
This was around the refresh question.
And just to kind of sum this up, like the goal here is to establish a balance between refreshing and net new because you need the net new to grow.
But the refresh helps you maintain your search volume and maintain the traffic and maintain the growth.

So we have to kind of do that like, you know, with franchise tax, maintaining that 5%, but continuing to create new content is the stuff that gives you that 36,000% increase from one week or one month.
to the next.
So a refresh cycle, like right currently how we're kind of handling it is, you know, we're doing this ad hoc refreshing as we see kind of things drop off.
If things are needed, then, you know, we have to kind of step back on some net new production to kind of handle those refreshes.
Although we could do a situation where we're scheduling like proactive refreshing.
We're doing it by cluster.
We're kind of strategically picking, you know, clusters to update.
The question really then becomes, like, how do we balance that continued growth with the maintenance that a library of almost 700 pages work requires of us?
So just to pressure test kind of like the assumptions we've made here just before we kind of coast toward the end here.
Basically, I think what's working is the high weekly output.
We've got good workflows that are producing accurate content.
That state-by-state and cluster approach is what's compounding.
Compounding, you know, so we see that there's opportunity there for refreshing.
And the idea of creating a new cluster, calibrating for it, and then scaling, that workflow is helping us stay aligned, but we're not slowing down.
It only slows us down insofar as it takes us to get that calibration article right, and then we're free to kind of go.

Like, lead tracking.
And clarity, which we've already discussed a little bit, but like, you know, we need to figure out a way to maybe reliably track that.
And then kind of that the sites organized in a way that makes sense for a user, but also for what we're trying to achieve with content.

Some things that we were thinking about maybe for the coming year is looking at ways we can help support you, like add in more interactive tools.
I know that's something that you talked about with the team previously, ways we can think about CTA optimization.
I know that like one of the strengths early on is that you have like a really concise CTA of like you had a flat fee.
Like it was really easy to kind of say you didn't have a lot of friction.
know that one I'm tracking that
When someone was trying to convert.

We do refreshes as needed.
You've got support on SEO and AI visibility.

And then, of course, we're doing performance monitoring both SEO and then we're monitoring AI visibility via Check That.
So this just allows us to kind of sustain momentum and keep refreshes prioritized within our current production capacity.
And then while we're, like, just, I want to kind of pause here just thinking about.
time.

Yeah, I mean, so, it's an interesting question because I actually expected to see more organic lead creation as our traffic's increased by multiples.
And I'll have to look and see exactly, I didn't have time this morning to look and see exactly how it's trended over time.
It feels anecdotally like we're getting more organic traffic, or sorry, more organic leads, leads from the organic channel.
And so, in that sense, like, I'm happy to keep this experiment going, and I believe that it will be, you know, I've, I think with some tweaks to our approach from a, like, they're
Literally making our website friendlier for conversion, I think would make a big difference.

Like, that's, I don't know exactly for sure.
We're going to add retargeting email again.
I mean, you may recall that I did that last year, Carrie.

And so now we actually have headcount against that.
So I'm hopeful that like those two things in conjunction will work better.
I mean, you can, if you guys have other things that you think we should be doing that I'm not aware of, we can talk about them.
The alternative is like, if this continues to evolve, we can add more capabilities in the middle of the year.
I'm sure you guys accept more money in the future as well.
Not just that renewal, but it's, you know.
Yeah, I mean, so I will, I do want to say like, we started like, from a very basic stage in building out the website.
And so I think the results that we've seen so far, just in terms of Carrie kept saying content compounds, and that's kind of like, our internal mantra of you really need that time.

Sounds good.
Yeah.
Sorry.
didn't understand that this was a conversation about the renewal packages and there was a big deck involved.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Review Q4 performance and align on strategy for the upcoming April renewal.](https://fathom.video/share/a9_Eejv-B4iAc_mhHSkXvrzuc9jg2wZL?tab=summary&timestamp=376.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Organic ROI is now clear:** Discarding unreliable GA form-start data, Discern's internal attribution confirms organic search has generated \~$130k in closed ARR from 13 customers.](https://fathom.video/share/a9_Eejv-B4iAc_mhHSkXvrzuc9jg2wZL?tab=summary&timestamp=714.0)
  - [**Strategy must shift to conversion:** With traffic growth achieved (5–6x), the focus must shift from content coverage to converting that traffic into more pipeline.](https://fathom.video/share/a9_Eejv-B4iAc_mhHSkXvrzuc9jg2wZL?tab=summary&timestamp=1480.0)
  - **Content refresh is critical for ROI:** Proactive refreshes are needed to maintain traffic on mature content clusters (e.g., Franchise Taxes, now at 5% growth) and keep articles visible to AI, which favors content \<6 months old.
  - [**Renewal options will be presented:** GrowthX will send a deck with options for a flat renewal or an expanded scope that adds a proactive refresh cadence and higher content velocity.](https://fathom.video/share/a9_Eejv-B4iAc_mhHSkXvrzuc9jg2wZL?tab=summary&timestamp=1700.0)

## Topics

### Performance & Attribution

  - [**Initial Goal:** Build organic presence against legacy competitors (CSC, CT Corp).](https://fathom.video/share/a9_Eejv-B4iAc_mhHSkXvrzuc9jg2wZL?tab=summary&timestamp=415.0)
  - [**Key Metrics:**](https://fathom.video/share/a9_Eejv-B4iAc_mhHSkXvrzuc9jg2wZL?tab=summary&timestamp=642.0)
      - [**Content:** 668 articles delivered (net new + refreshes).](https://fathom.video/share/a9_Eejv-B4iAc_mhHSkXvrzuc9jg2wZL?tab=summary&timestamp=642.0)
      - [**SEO:** 757 pages ranking, 5–6x traffic growth.](https://fathom.video/share/a9_Eejv-B4iAc_mhHSkXvrzuc9jg2wZL?tab=summary&timestamp=642.0)
      - **AI Visibility:** \#2 overall, ahead of CSC.
  - [**Attribution Clarification:**](https://fathom.video/share/a9_Eejv-B4iAc_mhHSkXvrzuc9jg2wZL?tab=summary&timestamp=714.0)
      - [**GA Form Starts (7k):** Discarded as an unreliable metric due to spam and Stripe Atlas partnership traffic.](https://fathom.video/share/a9_Eejv-B4iAc_mhHSkXvrzuc9jg2wZL?tab=summary&timestamp=714.0)
      - [**Discern's Internal Attribution:** Confirms organic search is a high-value channel.](https://fathom.video/share/a9_Eejv-B4iAc_mhHSkXvrzuc9jg2wZL?tab=summary&timestamp=759.0)
          - [**Method:** "How did you hear about us?" question on all forms.](https://fathom.video/share/a9_Eejv-B4iAc_mhHSkXvrzuc9jg2wZL?tab=summary&timestamp=759.0)
          - [**Results:** \~$270k pipeline → \~$130k closed ARR from 13 customers.](https://fathom.video/share/a9_Eejv-B4iAc_mhHSkXvrzuc9jg2wZL?tab=summary&timestamp=784.0)
          - [**Performance:** Inbound organic leads close 2x faster than average and have a 50% higher win rate than sales-driven channels.](https://fathom.video/share/a9_Eejv-B4iAc_mhHSkXvrzuc9jg2wZL?tab=summary&timestamp=926.0)

### Strategic Shift: From Coverage to Conversion

  - [**Problem:** Traffic growth has outpaced lead generation.](https://fathom.video/share/a9_Eejv-B4iAc_mhHSkXvrzuc9jg2wZL?tab=summary&timestamp=1765.0)
  - [**New Focus:** Convert the existing traffic into more pipeline.](https://fathom.video/share/a9_Eejv-B4iAc_mhHSkXvrzuc9jg2wZL?tab=summary&timestamp=1480.0)
  - [**Proposed Tactics:**](https://fathom.video/share/a9_Eejv-B4iAc_mhHSkXvrzuc9jg2wZL?tab=summary&timestamp=1653.0)
      - [More bottom-of-funnel content (e.g., comparison pages).](https://fathom.video/share/a9_Eejv-B4iAc_mhHSkXvrzuc9jg2wZL?tab=summary&timestamp=491.0)
      - [CTA optimization.](https://fathom.video/share/a9_Eejv-B4iAc_mhHSkXvrzuc9jg2wZL?tab=summary&timestamp=1653.0)
      - [Dedicated conversion landing pages.](https://fathom.video/share/a9_Eejv-B4iAc_mhHSkXvrzuc9jg2wZL?tab=summary&timestamp=1653.0)
      - [Interactive tools.](https://fathom.video/share/a9_Eejv-B4iAc_mhHSkXvrzuc9jg2wZL?tab=summary&timestamp=1653.0)
      - [Improved lead tracking.](https://fathom.video/share/a9_Eejv-B4iAc_mhHSkXvrzuc9jg2wZL?tab=summary&timestamp=1619.0)

### Content Refresh Strategy

  - [**Challenge:** Balance net-new content for growth with refreshes for maintenance.](https://fathom.video/share/a9_Eejv-B4iAc_mhHSkXvrzuc9jg2wZL?tab=summary&timestamp=1501.0)
  - [**Rationale:**](https://fathom.video/share/a9_Eejv-B4iAc_mhHSkXvrzuc9jg2wZL?tab=summary&timestamp=1407.0)
      - [Mature clusters (e.g., Franchise Taxes) see growth level off (\~5%), requiring refreshes to maintain traffic.](https://fathom.video/share/a9_Eejv-B4iAc_mhHSkXvrzuc9jg2wZL?tab=summary&timestamp=1407.0)
      - AI visibility favors content \<3–6 months old, making refreshes essential for defensibility.
  - [**Proposed Solution:** Shift from ad-hoc to a proactive, scheduled refresh cadence.](https://fathom.video/share/a9_Eejv-B4iAc_mhHSkXvrzuc9jg2wZL?tab=summary&timestamp=1533.0)

### Renewal & Spend

  - [**Context:** Organic channel payback is currently \>12 months.](https://fathom.video/share/a9_Eejv-B4iAc_mhHSkXvrzuc9jg2wZL?tab=summary&timestamp=1115.0)
  - [**Discern's Plan to Accelerate ROI:**](https://fathom.video/share/a9_Eejv-B4iAc_mhHSkXvrzuc9jg2wZL?tab=summary&timestamp=1740.0)
      - [Add retargeting email automation (now with dedicated headcount).](https://fathom.video/share/a9_Eejv-B4iAc_mhHSkXvrzuc9jg2wZL?tab=summary&timestamp=1815.0)
      - [Explore making the product more self-service.](https://fathom.video/share/a9_Eejv-B4iAc_mhHSkXvrzuc9jg2wZL?tab=summary&timestamp=1800.0)
  - [**GrowthX Renewal Options:**](https://fathom.video/share/a9_Eejv-B4iAc_mhHSkXvrzuc9jg2wZL?tab=summary&timestamp=1700.0)
      - [**Flat Renewal:** Sustain current momentum with 25 PSEO articles/week and ad-hoc refreshes.](https://fathom.video/share/a9_Eejv-B4iAc_mhHSkXvrzuc9jg2wZL?tab=summary&timestamp=1700.0)
      - [**Expanded Scope:** Increase content velocity and add a proactive refresh cadence.](https://fathom.video/share/a9_Eejv-B4iAc_mhHSkXvrzuc9jg2wZL?tab=summary&timestamp=1890.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**GrowthX:** Send renewal options deck to Discern.](https://fathom.video/share/a9_Eejv-B4iAc_mhHSkXvrzuc9jg2wZL?tab=summary&timestamp=1915.0)
  - [**Discern:** Review deck and analyze ROI data with Danny (GTM Engineer).](https://fathom.video/share/a9_Eejv-B4iAc_mhHSkXvrzuc9jg2wZL?tab=summary&timestamp=1936.0)
  - [**Discern:** Loop in Danny to improve site tracking and data access for GrowthX.](https://fathom.video/share/a9_Eejv-B4iAc_mhHSkXvrzuc9jg2wZL?tab=summary&timestamp=1949.0)

Analyze organic leads vs traffic; share findings w/ Andi + Carrie
Loop in Danny re: attribution; grant GrowthX access to Discen attribution data
